title: TeratoPHEN: Harnessing existing and newly developed systems to more rapidly identify and better delineate the full phenotype of known or suspected teratogens
author: Yates, Laura Michelle
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Demonstration project 2.5.4 aims to investigate whether existing and newly developed pharmacovigilance and clinical genetics data collection systems can be harnessed to more rapidly identify and better delineate the full phenotype of known or suspected teratogens.  
 This study, sub-study 2 (S2) will investigate reporting and recording of information relating to maternal medication use in pregnancy, genetic testing and offspring phenotype in a UK dataset held within Decipher, a global clinical genetics research database. It will also investigate what amendments to the Decipher database would be required to optimise the database for collection of pregnancy pharmacovigilance data with a focus on deep phenotyping of dysmorphology, congenital anomaly and follow-up of longer term outcomes.
steps_raw:
Demonstration project 2.5.4 aims to investigate whether existing and newly developed pharmacovigilance and clinical genetics data collection systems can be harnessed to more rapidly identify and better delineate the full phenotype of known or suspected teratogens.  
 This study, sub-study 2 (S2) will investigate reporting and recording of information relating to maternal medication use in pregnancy, genetic testing and offspring phenotype in a UK dataset held within Decipher, a global clinical genetics research database. It will also investigate what amendments to the Decipher database would be required to optimise the database for collection of pregnancy pharmacovigilance data with a focus on deep phenotyping of dysmorphology, congenital anomaly and follow-up of longer term outcomes.
